summary: Julia Evans shares follow-up notes and resources from her CUSEC conference talk. The post covers two main topics from the talk: debugging techniques and understanding computer performance. She recommends specific tools like strace, dtrace, wireshark, and mitmproxy for debugging, and profilers like visualvm and YourKit for performance analysis. The post serves as a curated reference list linking to her own blog posts and external resources.
key_points: strace on Linux and dtrace on OS X are essential debugging tools that every developer should learn
Network analysis tools like ngrep, mitmproxy, and wireshark make debugging network issues accessible and even fun
Understanding computer performance requires hands-on tools and profiling, not just assumptions about what's fast or slow
A collection of real debugging stories and performance investigations makes these concepts concrete and learnable
money_quotes: on Linux, learn strace. It's the best.
wireshark gives you a really nice graphical interface for network spying. It's fun.
key_insight: Debugging and performance analysis become approachable when you know the right tools — strace, dtrace, wireshark, and profilers — and have concrete stories showing how they solve real problems.
